title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: members, contributors, leaders pledge make participation community harassment-free experience everyone, regardless age, body size, visible invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity expression, level experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, caste, color, religion, sexual identity orientation. pledge act interact ways contribute open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, healthy community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Examples behavior contributes positive environment community include: Demonstrating empathy kindness toward people respectful differing opinions, viewpoints, experiences Giving gracefully accepting constructive feedback Accepting responsibility apologizing affected mistakes, learning experience Focusing best just us individuals, overall community Examples unacceptable behavior include: use sexualized language imagery, sexual attention advances kind Trolling, insulting derogatory comments, personal political attacks Public private harassment Publishing others’ private information, physical email address, without explicit permission conduct reasonably considered inappropriate professional setting

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders responsible clarifying enforcing standards acceptable behavior take appropriate fair corrective action response behavior deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, harmful. Community leaders right responsibility remove, edit, reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, contributions aligned Code Conduct, communicate reasons moderation decisions appropriate.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct applies within community spaces, also applies individual officially representing community public spaces. Examples representing community include using official e-mail address, posting via official social media account, acting appointed representative online offline event.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Instances abusive, harassing, otherwise unacceptable behavior may reported community leaders responsible enforcement wjakethompson@gmail.com. complaints reviewed investigated promptly fairly. community leaders obligated respect privacy security reporter incident.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community leaders follow Community Impact Guidelines determining consequences action deem violation Code Conduct:

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Use inappropriate language behavior deemed unprofessional unwelcome community. Consequence: private, written warning community leaders, providing clarity around nature violation explanation behavior inappropriate. public apology may requested.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: violation single incident series actions. Consequence: warning consequences continued behavior. interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, specified period time. includes avoiding interactions community spaces well external channels like social media. Violating terms may lead temporary permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: serious violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior. Consequence: temporary ban sort interaction public communication community specified period time. public private interaction people involved, including unsolicited interaction enforcing Code Conduct, allowed period. Violating terms may lead permanent ban.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Community Impact: Demonstrating pattern violation community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment individual, aggression toward disparagement classes individuals. Consequence: permanent ban sort public interaction within community.

title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
text: Code Conduct adapted Contributor Covenant, version 2.1, available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct.html. Community Impact Guidelines inspired [Mozilla’s code conduct enforcement ladder][https://github.com/mozilla/inclusion]. answers common questions code conduct, see FAQ https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations available https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

title: Custom color ramps — color_ramp
text: color ramp functions create color scales can used making ggplot2 plots gt tables. Color ramps based palette_wjake color palette.

title: Custom color ramps — color_ramp
code: make_color_pal(colors, bias = 1)  ramp_blue(output, end = "#FFFFFF")  ramp_yellow(output, end = "#FFFFFF")  ramp_yelblu(output, mid = "#F0F0F0")  ramp_orgblu(output, mid = "#999999")

title: Custom color ramps — color_ramp
text: colors colors interpolate; must valid argument     col2rgb(). bias positive number.  Higher values give widely spaced     colors high end. output Colors pull color ramp. Numbers range 0-1, normalized sliding scale color ramp. end end color base color fade . mid pre-made diverging palettes, color midpoint scale.

title: Custom color ramps — color_ramp
text: make_color_pal() returns function accepts numeric vector values 0 1 returns character vector hex color codes. ramp_blue(), ramp_yellow(), ramp_yelblu(), ramp_orgblu() return character vector hex color codes.

title: Custom color ramps — color_ramp
text: make_color_pal() can used create color ramp function set valid colors. ramp_blue(), ramp_yellow(), ramp_yelblu() pre-made color ramps based blue yellow colors palette_wjake color palette. ramp_orgblu() pre-made color ramp based first two colors palette_okabeito color palette.

title: Custom color ramps — color_ramp
code: new_ramp <- make_color_pal(c("red", "grey", "blue")) new_ramp(seq(0, 1, length.out = 10)) #>  [1] "#FF0000" "#F02A2A" "#E25454" "#D37E7E" "#C5A8A8" "#A8A8C5" "#7E7ED3" #>  [8] "#5454E2" "#2A2AF0" "#0000FF"  # Pre-made palettes ramp_blue(seq(0, 1, by = 0.2)) #> [1] "#FFFFFF" "#CCEBF0" "#99D8E2" "#65C5D3" "#32B2C5" "#009FB7" ramp_yellow(seq(0.2, 1, length.out = 5)) #> [1] "#FEF7E0" "#FEEFC1" "#FEE7A3" "#FEDF84" "#FED766"

title: Text and Number Formatting — formatting
text: formatting functions used format numerical values consistent manner. useful printing numbers inline text, well formatting tables.

title: Text and Number Formatting — formatting
code: fmt_digits(   x,   digits = 1,   big_mark = ",",   min_value = -Inf,   max_value = Inf,   sub_threshold = 1/(10^digits),   keep_boundary = FALSE,   ... )  fmt_count(x, ...)  fmt_corr(x, digits = 3, ...)  fmt_prop(x, digits = 2, ...)  fmt_pct(x, digits = 0, ...)  fmt_prop_pct(x, digits = 0, ...)

title: Text and Number Formatting — formatting
text: x Number formatted. digits Number decimal places retain. big_mark Character used every 3 digits separate thousands. min_value minimum value x can take. max_value maximum value x can take. sub_threshold threshold use replacing value extremely close min_value max_value. default, determined digits specified. keep_boundary Whether preserve true values boundaries (.e., min_value max_value). example digits = 3, min_value = 0, keep_boundary = FALSE, value exactly equal 0 become "<.001". keep_boundary = TRUE, value 0 remain "0.000", small values (e.g., 0.000001) continue replaced "<.001". ... Additional arguments passed scales::number_format() fmt_digits(). See Details additional information.

title: Text and Number Formatting — formatting
text: updated character object length x.

title: Text and Number Formatting — formatting
text: fmt_digits() wrapper scales::number_format() prints number specified number digits, suppressing values close minimum maximum values necessary. Several helper functions provided wrap fmt_digits() common patterns min_value, max_value, digits. fmt_count() used formatting integer values. Prints whole numbers decimals. fmt_corr() used format correlations similar indices bounded [-1, 1]. default, values report 3 decimal places, leading 0 removed required APA (2020; section 6.36). fmt_prop() used format proportions similar indices bounded [0, 1]. Similar fmt_corr(), leading 0s removed. default, 2 decimal places reported. fmt_pct() used format percentage values bounded [0, 100]. default, decimal places reported. fmt_prop_pct() formats proportions bounded [0, 1] percentages. , first take x * 100 apply fmt_pct().

title: Text and Number Formatting — formatting
text: American Psychological Association. (2020). Publication manual American Psychological Association (7th ed.). doi:10.1037/0000165-000

title: Title — gt_theme_apa

title: Title — gt_theme_apa
text: data gt table data object. dec_dig number decimal places round numeric values. corr_dig number decimal places round numeric values constrained [-1, 1]. fmt_extreme Logical indicator whether values close extremes suppressed (e.g., .000001 becomes <.001). bg_color background color table. font font use table. font_size base font size table. ... Additional options passed gt::tab_options()

title: Title — gt_theme_apa
text: object class gt-tbl.

title: Title — gt_theme_apa
code: gt::gt(head(penguins)) |>   gt_theme_apa() |>   gt::fmt_number(year, sep_mark = "", decimals = 0)     species       island       bill_len       bill_dep       flipper_len       body_mass       sex       year     Adelie Torgersen 39.1 18.7 181  3,750  male 2007 Adelie Torgersen 39.5 17.4 186  3,800  female 2007 Adelie Torgersen 40.3 18.0 195  3,250  female 2007 Adelie Torgersen NA NA NA NA NA 2007 Adelie Torgersen 36.7 19.3 193  3,450  female 2007 Adelie Torgersen 39.3 20.6 190  3,650  male 2007

title: Okabe-Ito color palette — palette_okabeito
text: Colorblind friendly palette taken originally described Okabe Ito (2008). original palette modified slightly make yellow visible white background, described Lüdecke et al. (2021). palette also includes color scales can used place ggplot2::scale_color_discrete() ggplot2::scale_fill_discrete().

title: Okabe-Ito color palette — palette_okabeito
text: character vector 8 hex color codes.

title: Okabe-Ito color palette — palette_okabeito
text: Lüdecke, D., Patil, ., Ben-Shachar, M. S., Wiernik, B. M., Waggoner, P., & Makowski, D. (2021). see: R package visualizing statistical models. Journal Open Source Software, 6(64), Article 3393. doi:10.21105/joss.03393 Okabe, M., & Ito, K. (2008). Color universal design (CUD): make figures presentations friendly colorblind people. https://jfly.uni-koeln.de/color/#pallet (Original work published 2002)

text: Color palette used theming https://wjakethompson.com. palette also includes color scales can used place ggplot2::scale_color_discrete() ggplot2::scale_fill_discrete().

title: Personal color palette — palette_wjake
text: character vector 5 hex color codes.

Round to a specified value — round_to
text: x numeric value round. accuracy accuracy round (.e., round nearest accuracy). direction direction round. nearest (default) round nearest accuracy, round nearest accuracy, round nearest accuracy, random randomly round nearest accuracy described Matthews & Harel (2011).

title: Round to a specified value — round_to
text: numeric rounded specified accuracy.

title: Round to a specified value — round_to
text: Matthews, G., & Harel, O. (2011). Data confidentiality: review methods statistical disclosure limitation methods assessing privacy. Statistics Surveys, 5, 1–29. doi:10.1214/11-SS074

title: Round to a specified value — round_to
code: round_to(15, accuracy = 7, direction = "nearest") #> [1] 14 round_to(15, accuracy = 7, direction = "up") #> [1] 21 round_to(20, accuracy = 7, direction = "down") #> [1] 14

title: Palette color scale — scale_palette
text: qualitative scale using color palette used https://wjakethompson.com. See palette_wjake details.

title: Palette color scale — scale_palette
text: palette character vector color palette use scale (e.g., palette_wjake, palette_okabeito). order Numeric vector listing order colors used. Default order palette vector. darken Relative amount scale darkened (positive values) lightened (negative values). alpha Alpha transparency level color. Default transparency. ... common discrete scale parameters: name, breaks, labels, na.value, limits, guide, aesthetics. See ggplot2::discrete_scale details.

title: Palette color scale — scale_palette
text: ScaleDiscrete object can added ggplot2::ggplot().

title: Palette color scale — scale_palette
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(penguins, aes(x = bill_len, y = flipper_len, color = species)) +   geom_point() +   scale_color_wjake() #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale #> range (`geom_point()`).   ggplot(penguins, aes(bill_len, fill = species)) +   geom_density(alpha = 0.7) +   scale_fill_okabeito(order = c(1, 2, 5)) #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing non-finite outside the scale range #> (`stat_density()`).

title: Personalized ggplot2 theme — theme_wjake
text: Based ggplot2::theme_minimal().

title: Personalized ggplot2 theme — theme_wjake
code: theme_wjake(   base_size = 11.5,   base_family = "Source Sans Pro",   header_family = NULL,   base_line_size = base_size/22,   base_rect_size = base_size/22,   ink = "black",   paper = "white",   accent = "#FED766",   continuous = ramp_blue(c(0.1, 1), end = "#FFFFFF"),   discrete = palette_wjake,   transparent = FALSE,   ... )

title: Personalized ggplot2 theme — theme_wjake
text: base_size base font size, given pts. base_family base font family header_family font family titles headers. default, NULL, uses theme inheritance set font. setting affects axis titles, legend titles, plot title tag text. base_line_size base size line elements base_rect_size base size rect elements ink, paper, accent colour foreground, background, accented elements respectively. continuous character vector valid colors interpolated continuous color scale. discrete character vector colors use discrete color scales. transparent Logical indicator whether background plot transparent. ... Additional parameters passed ggplot2::theme().

title: Personalized ggplot2 theme — theme_wjake
text: theme object can added ggplot2::ggplot().

title: Personalized ggplot2 theme — theme_wjake
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2)  ggplot(penguins, aes(x = bill_len, y = flipper_len)) +   geom_point(aes(color = species), na.rm = TRUE) +   labs(     x = "Bill length (mm)",     y = "Flipper length (mm)",     title = "Seminal ggplot2 scatterplot example",     subtitle = "A plot that is only useful for demonstration purposes",     caption = "Brought to you by 🐧",     color = "Species"   ) +   theme_wjake() } # }

title: X & Y scales for percent and comma labels — xy_scales
code: library(ggplot2) set.seed(1234)  ggplot() +   geom_point(aes(x = sample(1000:9999, size = 100), y = runif(100, 0, 1))) +   scale_x_comma() +   scale_y_percent(breaks = seq(0, 1, by = .2))
